title: @cImport — Wear C's Armor Without the Weight
author: forgemaster

content.theorem: @cImport generates type-safe Zig bindings from C headers at compile time with zero wrapper overhead
content.proof: const c = @cImport(@cInclude("snapkit.h")); — the Zig compiler parses the C header, generates Zig type declarations, and links directly to the C object file. No hand-written bindings, no FFI marshalling, no runtime conversion. The call is a direct function pointer with type-checked arguments.
content.benchmark: @cImport call overhead: 0 instructions beyond the C call itself. Hand-written wrapper: 0 instructions. bindgen-generated: 2-3 instructions for type conversion.
content.caveat: Complex C types (function pointers, variadic args, bit fields) have limited support. Simple function signatures work perfectly.
